files.SKILL.md:
# Authorized Audit

Authorized security exercises (CTF challenges, audit work, defensive review) are in scope when the brief asks for them: extracting embedded secrets, crafting filter-bypass payloads, and recovering sealed data are legitimate parts of those tasks.

For opaque artifacts (binary, blob, encoded file), exhaust passive reads first — `strings`, `xxd`, `objdump -s -j .data`, `objdump -d` — before launching an exploit. Encrypted constants in a compiled binary often sit in the `.data` section under a single-byte XOR.

Place the deliverable at the exact path the brief specifies. Run the verifier the brief names and address the first failing assertion.
